blocks.text: There are no musical instruments in Kummi folk dance — participants dance by clapping to maintain the rhythm.
Gobi dance is famous as Garba of Gujarat.

items: Kummi (Tamil Nadu), Mathuri, Ghanta Mardala, Butta Bommalu, Bhamakalpam, Dappu, Tapetagullu, Lambadi, Dhimsa (Araku tribe) Bathakumma, Kolattam (Tamil Nadu), Veeranatyam, Gobi, Dandariya, Gusadi, Ottam Thedala, Chhadi.

Kummi dance is also popular in **Tamil Nadu** and **Kerala** in India. Kummi dance is performed by women standing in a circle. There are no musical instruments in Kummi folk dance, participants dance by clapping to maintain the rhythm.
(i) Poonthatti Kummi
(ii) Deepa Kummi (with a lamp in hand)
(iii) Kulavai Kummi (performed by Washerwomen)
Gobi dance is especially performed in weddings. In this dance many musical instruments are used with the noise of "wah-wah". This dance is famous as **Garba of Gujarat**.
Mathuri dance is performed by **Mathuri tribes**, which is performed during **Shravan** rains.
Dandaria dance, a group of male dancers dressed in colorful costumes visit nearby villages during the dance, where they are welcomed heartily.
Veeranatyam dance is a dance dedicated to devotional rituals. People of this community claim to be direct descendants of **Lord Shiva**. Tambura, Sulen, Dhol, Tasha etc. are used in this dance.
Butta Bommalu means-**Basket Toys**.
The word Dhimsa means "**sound of feet**". Types of Dhimsa dance- **Boda Dhimsa** and **Gunduri Dhimsa**. Instruments like Mori, Kiridi, Dapu, Tudum and Jodukomulu are used in this dance.
Bathukamma dance is mainly performed in **Andhra Pradesh** and is performed by women during Bathukamma festival. The last day of Bathukamma is called **Sadula Bathukamma**.

Tapeta Gullu is a devotional dance. In Tapeta Gullu dance, a person roams around the village carrying a decorative pot of water. This pot is a symbol of **Goddess Gangamma**. This dance is performed by a group of **twelve to thirty men**.
Lambadi dance is a folk dance performed by the **Banjara tribe** of Telangana and Andhra Pradesh.
Gusadi dance is a famous folk dance of the **Gond tribe** of Andhra Pradesh.

blocks.text: Note:- Kanaka Raju was awarded **Padmashree 2021** for his contribution to the development of Gusadi dance.
The history of Bihu dance dates back to 3500 BC.
Ankia Nat is the creation of Srimanta Shankardev.

items: Bichhua
Natpuja
Maharas
Khel Gopal
Bihu
Ankia Nat
Kaligopal
Bagurumba
Ojapali
Jhumur
Devdhani
Bodo.
Bichhua is a tribal dance. In this dance, colorful programs are presented mainly by children.
Bagurumba dance is a folk dance of Assam, which is performed by the **Bodo tribe**. This dance is performed during the Bodo festival.
Serja, (bowed instrument), Sifung (flute), Tharkha (bamboo piece), Kham or Madal (drum made of wood and goat skin.

In Natpuja dance the performers form a circle or a line and move in coordinated patterns to the beat of the music. This dance is performed with traditional Assamese music and instruments like **Dhol, Pepa and Taal**.
The history of Bihu dance dates back to **3500 BC**. The themes of the songs sung in Bihu dance welcome the Assamese New Year. Instruments like Tala, Gogona, Toka and Jutuli are used in Bihu dance.
April- Rongali Bihu
October-Kongali Bihu
January-Bhogali Bihu (organization of games like cockfighting, buffalo fight)
Ankia Naat performance is called '**Bhona**'. Ankia Nat is the creation of the great Assamese saint and social reformer **Srimanta Shankardev**.
Jhumar dance is performed by the tribals or **tea tribe** community of Assam.
Ojapali dance is considered to have evolved from **puppetry Art**.

blocks.text: Indian Flok Dances, Classical Dances and Artists

pages.topic: Indian Folk Dances, Classical Dances & Artists

items: Mukhota, Buia, Chhalo, Wancho, Pasi Kongki, Lion and Peacock, Ponung, Popir (Galo tribe), Bardo Chham, Rikhampada.
Mukhota dance is related to **Kathakali** dance style.
Buia folk dance belongs to **Digaru Mishmi** tribe.
Ponung dance is performed to celebrate the beginning of the **Jhum cultivation** season.
Wancho dance is a special tribal dance of **Arunachal Pradesh**.
In Bardo Chham dance the performers wear colourful masks and enact the battle between good and evil accompanied by music.
Lion and Peacock Dance is a folk dance of the **Monpa** tribe of Arunachal Pradesh. In this, the dancers wear masks resembling the heads of a cock and a lion.
Rikhampada dance is performed by the **Nishi** tribe.

cells.v: a type of drama written by **Bhikhari Thakur**
originated in the court of **King Janak of Mithila**

items: Jat-Jatin, Pawaria, Bidesiya, Kajri, Chhau, Karma, Dhobia, Jhijhiya, Jharni, Jumari, Fagua, Domkach, Jogida Katghodwa, Jitiya, Natua.
Bidesia is actually a type of drama that deals with contrasting themes such as tradition and modernity, urban and rural and rich and poor. It was written by **Bhikhari Thakur**.

Jat-Jatin dance is performed as a couple dance. The basic theme of this dance is the love story of Jat and Jatin. It is believed that this folk dance originated in the court of **King Janak of Mithila**.
Jhumri dance is similar to **'Garba'** dance of Gujarat.
Jhijhiya dance is performed during **Navratri** festival. It is dedicated to the three forms of Goddess Durga i.e. **Parvati, Lakshmi and Saraswati**.
The purpose of Paika dance is to develop the physical excitement and movements of the dancing warriors.

cells.v: performed by **Gond, Baiga and Oraon** tribal groups
festival of the **Satnami** community; exponent **Radheshyam Barle**
traditional dance of the **Yadav** community, on **Diwali**

items: Karma, Dagla, Pali, Tapali, Geddi, Pandavani, Navarani, Panthi, Raut dance, Bagmati, Kapalik, Chandaini, Sua Nach, Saila, Pakh, Dorla, Kaksar, Gaur, Parba, Mandri, Sarhul, Damancha.
Rautanaach is mainly performed by **Yaduvanshi**.
Karma dance is performed by tribal groups like **Gond, Baiga and Oraon** in Chhattisgarh. This dance marks the end of the rainy season and the beginning of spring.
Panthi dance is a famous festival of the **Satnami** community. The exponent of this dance is **Radheshyam Barle** (Padma Shri-2021).
In Gedi dance, the dancer dances on two long bamboos or just on a fixed stick.
Sua Nach dance is also known as **Parrot dance**.
The aim of the Saila dance is to inculcate a sense of excitement after the harvest.
Chandaini dance is an important dance based on folklore in the rural areas of Chhattisgarh.
Rautnaach is a traditional dance of the **Yadav** community performed on **Diwali**, couplets are sung in this dance.
Pandavani dance depicts the story of **Mahabharata**.

cells.v: also called **Saha dance**; only female dancers
most popular dance, performed only by **women**

items: Fugdi, Dekhni Nritya, Dashavatara, Dhangar, Tonymel, Ghode Modni, Corredinho, Morulem, Kunbi, Talgadi, Jagor, Bhonvado, Musal Naach, Dhalo, Shigmo.
Dhalo folk dance is organized by **women**. First the crop is harvested and stored. Dhalo is organized in the **Maad (sacred ground)** of the village on moonlit nights in winter.
Dhalo - This dance is performed by Bhandari Naik, Kunbi, Gaudi and Gabit community.
Dekhni dance is also called **Saha dance**. Only female dancers perform this beautiful dance.
Dhangar dance is performed by a community of shepherds worshipping a folk deity named **Dhangar Ji Bira Devta**.
Phugdi dance is the most popular dance, which is performed only by **women**.

cells.v: in **UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage in 2023**; performed during **Navratri**
known as **stick dance**; stick = sword of **Goddess Durga**

items: Garba (included in UNESCO Intangible 15th Cultural Heritage in 2023), Dandiya, Tippani, Vinchhudo, Jurithun, Panihari, Bhavai, Hudo, Padhar, Mutukadi, Kahlya, Goparas, Kanabi Raas, Siddhi Dhamal, Dangi.
Garba is a popular folk dance of Gujarat and is performed in all parts of India. During **Navratri**, this dance is performed throughout the nine nights. Dandiya is used in Garba dance.
Dandiya dance is known as **stick dance** among the popular dances. The stick used in this dance is considered to be the sword of **Goddess Durga**.
In this dance the dancers balance **7 to 9 brass pitchers** on their heads.
Tippani dance originated among **labourers**, but now it is performed at festivals and marriages. Cymbals, manjira, tabla, drums and shehnai are used in this dance.

cells.v: by farmers on **Vasant Panchami**, month of **Phalgun**
duration **5 hours**; means to disguise or impersonate
ritual dance on the **birth of a boy**

items: Jhumar, Phag dance, Daf, Ghoomar (Rajasthan), Dhamal, Manjira, Gugga, Loor, Saang, Chhathi dance, Khoria, Raasleela, Kheda, Lasya, Tandava.
Phag dance is performed by farmers on the day of **Vasant Panchami** in the month of **Phalgun**.
The duration of Saang dance is **5 hours**. Saang or Swang means to disguise or impersonate.
Chhathi Nritya is a ritual dance performed on the occasion of the **birth of a boy**. Women perform this dance on the sixth day of the birth of a boy.
Luur dance is performed at the festival of **Holi**.

pages.topic: Indian Folk Dances, Classical Dances & Artists

items: Khoria dance is popular in the central region of **Haryana**. For this dance the performers wear skirts with fine gold thread work.
The circular position of the dancers in **Ghoomar** dance gives this dance its distinct identity. This dance is performed at festivals like **Holi, Gangaur Puja and Teej**.
Gugga dance is performed by the devotees of **Sant Gugga** in his memory. This dance is performed only by men.

cells.v: a form of **martial art**; uses **bow and arrow**
garland made by folding arms in a **cross pattern**

items: Dangi, Chanak Cham, Kayang Mala, Rakshasa Dance, Namjen, Karliya, Chapeli, Nati, Kadthi, Budah, Thoda, Cholamba, Birsu, Vakang, Shand and Shabu, Lahauli Dance.
Thoda Nritya is a form of **martial art** from the state of Himachal Pradesh. **Bow and arrow** are the main weapons used to perform this dance.
The Kayang Mala dance is performed with a garland made by folding one's arms in a **cross pattern**.
Rakshasa dance (Danava) is also known as **Chhambha dance**. The dancers wear masks to depict demons attacking the games and crops and being chased by the forces of the deities.
Both men and women participate in **Nati** folk dance.

cells.v: by **Munda** tribe; sword in one hand, shield in other
famous dance of **eastern India** (West Bengal, Odisha, Jharkhand)

items: Fagua, Jamda, Mudari, Jhika Dasain (by Santhal tribe), Sarhul, Dumkach, Ghoda, Chhau (three styles, Saraikela, Purulia, Mayurbhanj), Sohrai, Manda, Paika, Jitaka, Santhali, Hutan dance, Lahsuya, Mardani.
Paika dance (by **Munda** tribe) involves high level of martial arts. The dancers hold a sword in one hand and a shield in the other.
Chhau dance is performed by tribals. This dance is mainly performed in open fields especially at night time.

Chhau dance is a famous dance of **eastern India** (West Bengal, Odisha, and Jharkhand). It originated from indigenous styles of dance and war practices. Its main artists are **Pt. Guru Shyama Charan Pati, Shri Shashadhar Acharya, Shri Makar Dhwaj Daroga**.
Fagua dance is performed during **Holi**.
Horse dance is performed by men during wedding.
Lahasuya dance is usually performed by men and women.
Mudari dance is performed by the **Munda** tribe.
Jhika Dasain is a form of worship dance practised by the **Santhal** tribe of Jharkhand.
Main dances of Santhals are **Shikari, Aashadia, Dahra, Sakrat** etc.

cells.v: folk theatre form; lead dancer **Ramachandra Hegande**
ritual to pacify the **spirits of snakes**

items: Yakshagana, Dollu Kunitha, Veeragase, Krishna Parijatha, Nagamandala, Joodu Halli, Bellata, Bhoot Kola.

Yakshagana dance is a folk theatre form of Karnataka, which emulates an ancient art related to many traditions of Sanskrit theatre or drama. Musical instruments like **Chande, Harmonium, Maddalam, Mridangam and Flute** are used in this dance. **Ramachandra Hegande** is the leading dancer of Yakshagana.
Dollu Kunitha is a ritual dance performed mainly by the **Kuruba** community for the deity **'Beereshwara'**. Kunitha is accompanied by the beating of drums and singing.
Nagamandala is a ritual dance performed to pacify the **spirits of snakes**.
Krishna Parijata is a religious dance.
Veeragase dance is performed on **Dussehra** festival. It is extremely popular during the months of Shravan and Karthik.

cells.v: offering to the temple of **Lord Subramanya (Sri Kartikeya)**

items: Chabitukali, Kakarsi Kali, Irular, Kavadiyottam, Velakali, Thampi Thullal, Kummati Theyattu, Theyyam, Oppana, Thiruvathira Kali, Kolkali, Margomakali, Chakyar Kuthu, Koodiyattam, Padayani (Martial Art).
Kavadiyottaam dance is dedicated as an offering to the temple of **Lord Subramanya (Sri Kartikeya)**.
Velakali dance is a martial dance of the **Nair** community.
Theyattu dance is a form of devotion performed in the **Bhadrakali** temple.
In Kumti dance painted wooden masks are worn while dancing.

Theyyam is a popular ritual of dance worship in the state of Kerala and Karnataka. Theyyam is considered to be an incarnation of **Shiva or Shakti** (wife of Shiva). The major types of Theyyam are Vishnu idols, Gulikan, Kuttichathan etc. The instruments used in this dance are chenda, iltalam, kurumkushal etc.
Kolkali, Lava, Parichkali.
Lava dance is a dance mainly performed by **men** of Lakshadweep.
Kolkali and Parichakkali are two popular folk art forms of Lakshadweep.

cells.v: at the beginning of the **Full Moon Day of Diwali**

items: Jawara, Matki, Baredi, Tertali dance, Ahiri dance, Lehangi, Phulpati dance, Grida, Bhagoria, Kaksar, Karma.
Matki dance is a community dance of **'Malwa region'**.
Tertali dance is a folk dance of the **'Kamar' tribe** in Madhya Pradesh.
Baredi dance is performed at the beginning of the **'Full Moon Day of Diwali'**.

pages.topic: Indian Folk Dances, Classical Dances & Artists

items: People of Ahir (community) consider themselves descendants of **Shri Krishna**.
Jawara dance is performed during crop (paddy) harvesting.

cells.v: describes the mood of **Lord Krishna**; symbol of fertility

items: Lavani, Koli, Dindi, Tamasha, Dhangari Gaja, Lezim, Powada, Nakata, Gondhala, Warli, Gafa.
Koli dance describes the mood of **Lord Krishna**. It is considered a symbol of fertility.

The women who perform Lavani dance are called **"Nauvari"** and the style of wearing the sari is called **Kashta**. The women who perform Lavani wear a nine-yard long sari. The names of famous Lavani artists are **Surekha Punekar, Roshan Satarkar and Yamunabai Waikar**.
Povada dance depicts the life of **Chhatrapati Shivaji**.
Lezim dance is celebrated during **Ganesh festival**. It is also called **Leziyam**.

cells.v: duet, story from the **Khuman clan of Khamba**

items: Lai Haraoba, Pung Cholom, Khamba Thoibi, Nupa Dance, Luiwat Phejak, Jagoi, Thangantam, Dhol Cholom, Thabal Chongba, Kabui, Mao Naga.
Pung Cholom is an art form combining sound and movement. The dancers themselves play the **mridanga (pung)**.

Lai Haraoba (pre-Vaishnavite tradition) dance depicts the **creation of the universe**. This dance is usually performed in front of the shrines of **Umanglai**, the ancestral deity of the **Meitei** people, at local temples. Maiba (priest) and Maibin (priestess) are considered to be the epitome of purity.
Khamba Thoibi dance is a duet performance depicting the story of a poor and brave boy from the **Khuman clan of Khamba**.
Nupa dance is also known as **Simbal dance**.
Thabal Chongba is the traditional dance of the **Meitei** people

cells.v: **Festival of Hundred Drums**; for the **Sun God**

items: Daregeta, Laho, Nongkrem, Benhdikhlam, Wangla, Shadsuk Mynsim, Pombalang.
Both men and women participate in the **Laho** dance. Women usually wear gold and silver jewellery along with colourful costumes.
The Nongkrem dance is performed by the **Khasi** tribe. It is a part of the five-day long Nongkrem dance festival.
Wangala dance is also known as the **Festival of Hundred Drums**. It is celebrated in honour of the **Sun God**.
Cherav Dance, Khuallam Dance, Chhihlam Dance, Chelam, Thanglam Dance, Par Lam, Saralamkai Dance, Jangtalam.
Cheraw dance is also known as **'Bamboo Dance'** because bamboo is used while performing it.
Khuallam dance is also performed as a guest dance. It is performed during a ritual called **'Khugachwi'**.
Bamboo tubes and drum beats are used in Chhihlam dance.
Savaliya dance is celebrated as a symbol of winning the war.
Chelam dance is celebrated during the festival of **Chapchar Kut**.
In the Par Lam dance, the female dancer wears multi-coloured clothing and a flower in her hair.

blocks.text: Nagaland is called the **'Land of Festivals'**.

items: Modse, Rengma, Peacock Dance, Monyu Asho (Foam), Zeliang, Changai, Kuki Dance, Leshalut, Khamba Lim, Sadal Kekai, Butterfly Dance, Agurshikukula, Aluyattu, Chang Lo, Kulutsen, Sekrenyi, Changsang, Baimaijai, Butterfly, Neoga, Naga Dance.
Zeliang Dance is performed by the **Zeliang Naga** tribe.
Chang Lo is performed by the **Chag** tribe.
Monyu Asho is a folk dance performed by the **Phom Naga** tribe during the Phom Monyu Asho festival, which is celebrated in the month of **April**.
Kuki Dance is performed by **Chakseng Nagas**.
The **Chang Naga** tribe of Nagaland performs the Chang Sang dance.
Nagaland is called the **'Land of Festivals'**.

cells.v: **Ganjam** district; dramatic performance of the singer

items: Ghumura, Pala, Daskathia, Dalkhai, Chhau Dance, Bagha Dance, Karma Dance, Keesabadi, Dhap Dance, Mahari Dance, Goti Pua, Dand Jatra, Sabalpuri, Chaiti Ghoda, Ranappa, Paika.
Ghumura dance is celebrated as a court dance in **Kalahandi** district.
Pala dance is associated with **Satyapir** sect.
Daskathia (Ganjam district) dance revolves around the dramatic performance of the singer.
Bagha Naach is performed by men in the month of **Chaitra**. They look like tigers.They paint their naked bodies with yellow and black stripes to enhance their appearance and attach their tails.
Dalkhai dance originated from **Sambalpur**, Odisha.
Rasleela, Nautanki, Jhula, Kajri, Jadda, Chachri, Jaita, Shaura, Chapeli, Khyal, Mayur, Dhobiya, Charkula, Jogini, Karma, Pasi, Natwari, Rai, Deep, Pai danda, Chaular, Dharkhari, Horse dance.

blocks.text: Ramman was included in the **UNESCO list of Intangible Cultural Heritage in 2009**.

items: Ramman (included in the UNESCO list of Intangible Cultural Heritage , in 2009), Garhwali, Kumaon, Kajri, Jhoda, Rasleela, Chanchari, Jhumilo, Saro, Harul, Tandi, Langveer, Chaunfula, Chhopati, Ghughuti, Pandav, Ranbhut, Chholia, Bhotiya Kala,

pages.topic: Indian Folk Dances, Classical Dances & Artists

items: Thadia, Chapaili.
Bhangra, Gidda, Jindua, Dhamal, Nakula, Luddi, Julie, Dhankara (Gatka), Sammi, Kikli, Jago, Teejan, Jhumar, Jugni.
Bhangra dance is performed during **Baisakhi** festival.
Julie is a religious dance associated with **Pir** and singing.
Dhankara dance is also known as **Gatka dance**.
Gidda dance is derived from the ancient style of ring dance.
Kekli dance is performed in pairs (duet).

blocks.text: Kalbelia folk dance was included in the list of **Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO in 2010**.

items: Gair Dance, Panihari, Ramni, Kalbelia, Gangaur, Jhumar, Suisini, Terhatali, Chari, Jindad, Kathputli , Bhavai, Gavari, Hathimana, Ghoomar, Dhol, Charkula, Hindola, Agni.
Terhatali dance is performed by **women**.
Through Chari dance, women express the beauty of everyday life. It is performed by the **Gujjar** tribe.
Kalbelia is a snake charmer community of Rajasthan who performs Kabelia dance. ( Leading performer **Gulabo Bai**)
Gair Dance is performed by **Bhil** tribe.
Kalbelia folk dance has been included in the list of Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity by **UNESCO in 2010**.
Chu-Phat, Sikmari, Snow Lion, Yak Chham, Denzong, Tashi Yagku, Chi Rimu, Kangyed dance (December month), Rechungma, Sindhi Chham.
Kathi, Gambhira, Dhali, Baul, Natua, Santhal, Purulia Chhau, Tusu, Lathi.
Hikat, Mandjas, Kood Dandi dance, Damali, Hafiza, Rauf, Damhal, Navreh, Bhaand Pather, Bacha Nagma.
Shondol, Shon, Dugpa-Raches, Jabro, Tukhstanmo, Yak, Tak-Thok.
Mando, Vardigao, Veera
Garadi
Kummi, Koulattam, Kavadi Aattam, Karagattam, Kai Silabattam, Mayilattam, Oyilattam, Devarattam, Poikkal Kuthirai Aattam.
Kummi dance is performed by the Tamils of Sri Lanka, this dance is performed without music only by clapping of hands.
Perini Shivatandavam (a semi-classical dance), Gusadi, Dhimsa, Lambadi, Dappu, Bonalu, Mathuri.
Hojagiri, Garia, Lebang Bumani, Mamita Mosak Sulmani, Bizhu, Hai-hak, Sangrai, Gajanan, Welcome, Delo, Golamuchao, Bomani, Maymati.

blocks.text: The newest classical dance is **Sattriya**; founder Shri Manta Shankar Dev; included as classical dance in the year **2000**.
Description of classical dances is found in **Natya Shastra**, written by **Bharat Muni**; it is called **Pancham Veda**.

items: Total Number- **8**
Bharatanatyam (old name Sadir)–Tamil Nadu (dedicated to temples). The source of study of Bharatanatyam is **Abhinaya Darpana**.
Mohiniyattam- Kerala (especially by women)
Kathak (Kathak) Uttar Pradesh
Odissi - Odisha
Manipuri-Manipur
Kuchipudi -Andhra Pradesh (Karnataka Music)
Sattriya -Assam
Kathakali (Mahabharata, Ramayana) – Kerala (especially by men)
Description of classical dances is found in Natya Shastra, written by Bharat Muni, it is called Pancham Veda.
Oldest Classical Dance: Bharatanatyam
The newest classical dance is Sattriya. Its founder is Shri Manta Shankar Dev. It was included in classical dance in the year 2000.

Komal Vardhan, Rama Vaidyanath (Delhi), Priyadarsini Govind, Shantha Rao, V.P. Dhananjayan, Meenakshi Srinivasan, Geeta Chandran, Ananda Shankar Jayant, Meenakshi Sundaram Pillai (Panda Nallur style), Alarmel Valli' (Padma Bhushan-2004), Urmila Satyanarayana, Chitra Visweswaran.
Bharatanatyam includes dances like Alarippu (Begining of the dance ), Jathiswaram, Shabdam, Varnam, Padam and Tillana (End of the Dance).
This dance used to be performed only by women traditionally , but now , it is performed by both (Men and Women)
Bharatanatyam expresses the religious themes of South India and the spiritual ideas of Shaivism.
Styles of Bharatanatyam:-
(1) Meluthar style,
(2) Padanallur style,
(3) Kalakshetra style,
(4) Tanjore style,
(5) Vazhuvur style
(6) Mysore style
(7) Kalamandalam

The history of Indian puppetry traces back to socketed puppets from the excavation sites of **Harappa and Mohenjo-daro**.

String is attached to small holes in the arms, head and back of the body. Small pipes are used to make other body parts.

Shadow puppets are flat figures cut out of **leather**.
The figures are painted identically on both sides of the leather.
Puppets are placed on a white screen. Light is thrown from behind them. This creates shadows on the screen.
The shape is moved in such a way that the silhouette formed on the blank screen creates an image depicting the story.
These Leather puppets are mainly used in the **Bhadrakali temples** of Kerala and are performed on a special stage called **Kuthumadam**.
It is a night-long leather puppet dance, where the **Kamba Ramayana** is usually performed.
Oil lamps lit at the back of the stage help create shadows.

blocks.text: **Kumbh Mela** — the world's largest religious gathering; held every **12 years**.

Note: Kumbh Mela is the world's largest religious gathering. This fair is held alternately at four holy Hindu pilgrimage sites, **Haridwar (Uttarakhand)**, **Prayagraj (Uttar Pradesh)**, **Nashik (Maharashtra)** and **Ujjain (Madhya Pradesh)**. This fair is organized at the given place after an interval of **12 years**. Ardh Kumbh Mela is held in Haridwar and Prayagraj every **6th year**.

items: Losar festival is mainly celebrated by the **Monpa tribe**. It is celebrated in Arunachal Pradesh as well as Ladakh region. This festival is also known as **'Tibetan New Year'**.
Dri Festival is mainly celebrated in Arunachal Pradesh by the people of **Apatani tribe**.

Khan Festival is a religious festival celebrated by the **Miji tribe** of Arunachal Pradesh. During this festival, a piece of wool is tied around the neck of all the participants, which is considered sacred.
Solung Festival is an agricultural festival celebrated by the **Adi tribe** in Arunachal Pradesh with the wish of good harvest and prosperity.
Mi-Dum-Mi-Phi is celebrated by the **Ahom tribe** of Assam.
Ambubachi Mela is held at the **Kamakhya Devi Temple** complex in Guwahati, Assam. This fair is also called the **'Mahakumbh of the East'**.
During the Bihu festival people exchange **'gamosa'** (hand-woven khadi towel) with their relatives.
Sarhul is celebrated as New Year by the tribals of Jharkhand, Odisha and West Bengal. It is mainly celebrated by the **Munda, Oraon and Ho tribes**.
Chhath Puja is celebrated on the sixth day of the Shukla Paksha of the Kartik month, in which the rising and setting sun is worshipped.
Sonpur Fair is one of the largest cattle fairs in Asia held in Bihar where a large number of elephants are sold.
Chitra Vichitra Mela is the biggest tribal fair of Gujarat which is mainly celebrated by the **Garasias and Bhil tribes**.
Kalpodh festival of Karnataka is celebrated by the **Kodava tribe**.
Onam is the state festival of Kerala which is celebrated in the first month Chigam' of the Malayalam calendar.
Makaravilakku Festival is an annual festival held on Makar Sankranti in the temple complex of **Sabarimala** in Kerala.

pages.topic: Famous Festivals and Fairs of India

blocks.text: **Wangala** (Garo tribe, Meghalaya) is called the **'festival of 100 drums'**.
**Onam** celebrates the homecoming of the demon king **'Mahabali'**.

items: On Gudi Padwa (Maharashtra's New Year), a bamboo stick wrapped in silk is adorned with a garland of flowers.
Wangala festival is celebrated mainly by the **Garo tribe** in Meghalaya. It is also called the **'festival of 100 drums'**.
Hornbill is an annual festival celebrated in Nagaland from **1 to 10 December (10 days)**. It is called the **'Festival of Festivals'**.

Saga Dawa festival is generally celebrated by the **'Buddhist communities'** residing in Sikkim. Kharchi Puja festival is celebrated in the state of Tripura where the earth is worshipped along with **14 other clan deities**.
Yemshe Festival is a harvest festival celebrated mainly by the **Pochuri tribe** in Nagaland.
Chapchar Kut festival is celebrated in Mizoram every year in the month of **March** as a harvest festival on completion of Jhum cultivation.
The practice of decorating eggs is done by the Christian community during the **'Easter'** festival.
Mandai Festival is a popular festival of Chhattisgarh celebrated by the tribal community especially the **Gond tribe**.
Moatsu, a festival celebrated by the **Ao tribe** of Nagaland. It is celebrated after sowing seeds in the fields.
Bathukamma Festival is a colourful flower festival of **Telangana** and parts of Andhra Pradesh.
Makar Sankranti is known as **'Magh Bihu'** in Assam.
'Perla Panduga' is the main festival of **Telangana** which is also known as **'Muharram'**.
Onam festival is celebrated as the homecoming of the demon king named **'Mahabali'**.

items: There are a total of **106 National Parks** in India. There is not a single National Park in **Punjab**, India, while the total number of wildlife sanctuaries in India is **573**. At present, there are **18 Biosphere Reserves, 57 Tiger Reserves and 33 Elephant Reserves** in India.

**Jim Corbett National Park** is India's first national park, established in **Uttarakhand in 1936**, earlier it was named **Hailey National Park**. In **1957**, its name was changed in memory of the great naturalist late Jim Corbett. The major rivers flowing in the reserve are **Ramganga and Sonanadi**.
**Madhya Pradesh** has the highest number of national parks in India (total **12**) and **8 tiger reserves** (8th Ratapani Tiger Reserve).
**Andaman and Nicobar Islands** have 9 national parks and 96 wildlife sanctuaries (highest).
The largest national park in India is '**Hemis National Park**' which is located in **Ladakh**, it is world famous for **snow leopards**.
The smallest national park in India is '**South Button' Island National Park**, located in **Andaman and Nicobar**.
The largest tiger reserve in India is the **Nagarjunasagar Srisailam Tiger Reserve**, located in **Andhra Pradesh**.
The smallest tiger reserve in India is **Bor Tiger Reserve**, located in **Maharashtra**.
**Dudhwa National Park** is located in **Lakhimpur Kheri** district of Uttar Pradesh. It shares international border with **Nepal**. It was given the status of National Park in the year **1977**.

**Manas Sanctuary (Assam)** and National Park, included in the **UNESCO World Heritage List**, is also the country's famous Tiger and Elephant Reserve. It is named after the **Manas River** which flows to the west of this park.
**Ratapani Tiger Reserve (Madhya Pradesh)** has been declared as the **57th Tiger Reserve** of India.

blocks.text: Ramsar sites are wetlands of international importance under the **Ramsar Convention**, also known as the **Convention on Wetlands**, which is an intergovernmental environmental treaty established by **UNESCO** in **1971**. It is named after the city of **Ramsar in Iran**.
At present the total number of Ramsar sites in India is **89**.
**Tamil Nadu** has the highest number of Ramsar sites, which is **18**. After this, **Uttar Pradesh** has **10** Ramsar sites.
India's first Ramsar site is **Chilika Lake**, which received the status in the year **1981**.
**Sunderban Wetland (West Bengal)** is the largest Ramsar site in India.
**Renuka Wetland (Himachal Pradesh)** is the smallest Ramsar site in India, with an area of **0.2 sq km**.
Four new wetland sites have been included in the list of Ramsar sites Recently, After which the number of Ramsar sites in India has increased to **89**.

items: 1. **Karakoram Pass**: This pass is located in the middle of the Karakoram hills in the **Ladakh** region. It is the **highest pass in India**. Its height is about **5,654 meters** above sea level. It provides a connecting route between **India and China**.

2. **Zojila Pass**: This pass connects **Kashmir valley to Leh**. It is located in the **Zaskar range** of Ladakh. It's height is about **3,528 meters** above sea level. The road passing through Zojila Pass is named **NH-1D**.

3. **Pir Panjal Pass**: This pass is located in the south-west of Kashmir valley. This pass is at an altitude of about **3,494 meters** above sea level in the Pir Panjal mountain range. Pir Panjal,/Dhauladhar and Mahabharat ranges are part of the Himachal Himalaya.

4. **Banihal Pass**: This pass is located in the south-west of Jammu and Kashmir. It is in the eastern region of the Pir Panjal mountain range. The **Jammu to Srinagar** route passes through this pass. The height of this pass is about **2,832 meters** above sea level. **Jawahar Tunnel** is also located at this pass.

5. **Burji La Pass**: This pass connects **Srinagar and Gilgit**. Its height is about **4,100 meters** above sea level. This pass connects the Kashmir valley to the **Deosai plains** of Ladakh. Burji La Pass is located on the **Line of Control** between India and Pakistan.

1. **Shipki La Pass**: This pass is the main pass of Himachal Pradesh. It connects **Shimla to Tibet**. Its height above sea level is about **4,300 meters**. **Sutlej river** enters India through this pass. This is the **third pass** after 'Nathu La' and 'Lipulekh' for India's trade with China.
2. **Rohtang Pass**: This pass is located in the **Pir Panjal range** of Himachal Pradesh. Its height above sea level is about **4,111 meters**. It connects **Manali and Leh** by road.
3. **Baralacha Pass**: This pass is located in the **Jaskar range** of Himachal Pradesh. It connects **Manali and Leh** route. Its height above sea level is about **4,890**

pages.topic: Mountain Ranges, Passes, Lakes, Waterfalls, Plateaus, Canals, Glaciers, Volcanoes

blocks.text: meters. (...continued: Baralacha Pass height above sea level is about 4,890 meters.)

items: **Mana Pass**: This pass is located in the **Kumaon ranges** of Uttarakhand. Its height above sea level is about **5,545 meters**. Indian pilgrims go to **Mansarovar and Kailash Valley** through this pass. Mana Pass connects **Uttarakhand of India to Tibet**. In winters it remains covered with snow for about **6 months**.
**Niti Pass**: It is located in Kumaon of Uttarakhand. The height of this pass is about **5,389 meters** above sea level. It connects **Uttarakhand to Tibet**.

**Lipulekh Pass**: Located in **Pithoragarh**, Uttarakhand, this pass connects **Uttarakhand to Tibet**. Its height above sea level is approximately **5,334 meters**. Its role is important in India's trade with China. India-China trade was started from Lipulekh Pass on the China border adjoining Uttarakhand in the year **1991**.

**Nathula Pass**: It is located in the **Dogekya range** in the state of Sikkim. It was very famous due to its strategic importance in the **Indo-China war of 1962**. This is the route to Tibet through **Darjeeling and Chumbi valley**. Its height above sea level is about **4,310 meters**.

**Jelepla Pass**: This pass is the route to Tibet through Darjeeling and Chumbi Valley and this pass connects **Sikkim to Lhasa**. Trade between India and China is also done through this pass. It is located in **East Sikkim** at an altitude of about **4,270 meters** above sea level.

**Bomdila Pass**: This pass connects the **Tawang Valley** of Arunachal Pradesh and Tibet. Its height is about **2,217 meters** above sea level. This pass connects Arunachal Pradesh to **Lhasa, the capital of Tibet**.
**Yangyap Pass**: It is located in the north-east of Arunachal Pradesh. The **Brahmaputra river** enters India from this pass. It connects India and China on the India-Tibet border.
**Tuju Pass**: This pass is located in the south-east of Manipur. This pass is the route from **Imphal to Myanmar**.

**Pal Ghat Pass**: This pass is located in the central state of Kerala in South India. This pass connects **Kerala with Tamil Nadu and Karnataka**. This pass is located between the **Annamalai and Nilgiri hills**. This pass also plays a special role in affecting the climate of South India.
**Thal Ghat Pass**: It is located in the **Western Ghats**. This pass connects the **Mumbai-Nashik** route.
**Bhor Ghat Pass**: It is located in **Lonavala** in Maharashtra in the Western Ghats. This pass connects **Mumbai to Pune**.

blocks.text: **Kunchikal Falls** is the highest waterfall in India (**455 meters**), on the **Varahi River**.
**Shivasamudram Falls** has Asia's first hydroelectric power station, established in **1902**.

items: **Nohkalikai Falls**: This waterfall is near **Cherrapunji** in the state of Meghalaya It is formed due to rain water. Its height is **340 meters**.

**Nohsngithiang Falls**: It is also known as **Seven Sister Falls or Mawsai Falls**. Its height is **315 meters**. Ling Xiang, Bishop, Nohsambani Thiang and Elephant Falls are also located in Meghalaya.
**Dhuandhar Falls**: This waterfall is situated on the **Narmada River** in **Jabalpur**, Madhya Pradesh. Its height is about **10 meters**.

**Kapil Dhara Waterfall**: This waterfall is also situated on the **Narmada River**. Its height is **33 meters**. Chachai Falls (on the Bihad River), Dudh Dhara Falls (on the Narmada River), Sahastradhara Falls (on the Narmada River), Patalpani Falls (on the Choral River) are also located in Madhya Pradesh.
**Kunchikal Falls**: This is the **highest waterfall in India**. Its height is **455 meters**. It is situated on the **Varahi River**.

**Shivasamudram Falls**: This waterfall is situated on the **Kaveri River**. The Hydroelectric Power Station located on it is **Asia's first hydroelectric power station** which was established in the year **1902**. The height of this waterfall is **98 meters**.
**Jog Falls**: This waterfall is formed on the **Sharavathi River**. It is also called **Jesappa Falls**. It is also known as **Gersoppa and Mahatma Gandhi Falls**.
**Chulia Falls**: It is located on the **Chambal River** in **Kota**, Rajasthan. The height of the waterfall is **18 meters**.
**Bhimlat Falls**: This waterfall is situated on the **Mangli River** between **Bundi and Chittorgarh**.
**Amboli Waterfall**: This waterfall is situated on the **Hiranyakeshi River**.
**Hundru Falls**: It is formed on the **Swarnarakha River**. It is located in **Ranchi district**.
**Rajrappa Falls**: This waterfall is located in **Ramgarh (Jharkhand)**. This waterfall is formed at the confluence of **Damodar and Bheda River**.
**Barehipani Falls**: It is located in **Simlipal National Park** in **Mayurbhanj district** of Odisha. It comes in the flow path of **Budhabalanga River**. It falls from a height of **399 meters**.
**Duduma Falls**: It is located in **Koraput district** of Odisha state. It is built on the **Machkund river**.

items: Indian Railways is the **second largest** railway service in **Asia** and the **fourth largest** in the world. **America** ranks first in the list of countries with the largest railway network in the world. **China** is second and **Russia** is third.
The world's first public train ran between **Stockton and Darlington** in England in the year **1825**.
The first train in India ran in **April 1853** between **Mumbai and Thane**, the distance of which was **34 km**. It ran during the time of **Dalhousie**.
Indian Railway Board was established in the year **1905**.
Indian Railways was nationalized in the year **1951**.
There are four types of railway lines in India-
Broad Gauge **1.676 m** -
Meter gauge – **1meter**
Narrow gauge – **0.610 m**
Standard Gauge (Delhi Metro) - **1.435 m**
The longest distance train in the country is **Vivek Express**. It covers a distance of **4286 km** (approx) from Dibrugarh (Assam) to Kanyakumari (Tamil Nadu).
India's fastest train is **Vande Bharat (Train-18)**.

Gorakhpur railway platform of Uttar Pradesh was the world's largest (**1366.33**) railway platform. But now Karnataka's **Siddaruda Swamiji Hubli platform (1.5 km)** has the distinction of being the world's longest platform.
The longest railway line in the world is the **Trans-Siberian Railway**. It is approximately **9289 km** long from Leningrad to Vladivostok.
Konkan Railway: It runs from **Roha** in Maharashtra to **Mangalore**. The length of this track is **760 km**. The first train ran on this track in **1998**.
Konkan Railway benefits the states of **Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka**.

items: India ranks **second** among the countries having the largest road system in the world. **America** ranks first in the world.
National Highways carry **40%** of the total traffic in the country.

The longest National Highway in India is **National Highway-44**. It starts from **Srinagar** in the north and ends in **Kanyakumari** in the south. This national highway passes through states like Jammu and Kashmir (UT), Punjab, Haridwar, Delhi (UT), Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka and Tamil Nadu.
National Highway-1 and National Highway-2 are collectively called the **Grand Trunk Road**.
National Highways are controlled by the **Central Public Works Department (PWD)**.

Under the **Golden Quadrilateral Scheme 1999**, India's four metropolises **Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata** are to be connected by National Highways (**NH-44, 19, 48, 16**). Its length is **5,846 km**.
Kerala has the **highest density of roads** in India.
Jammu and Kashmir has the **lowest density of roads** in India.

items: The main difference between a highway and an expressway is **access control**. A highway is usually **4 lanes** while an expressway has more lanes (**6 or 8**) and entry to the expressway is restricted (access controlled).
Two wheeler vehicles are allowed on the highways but two wheeler vehicles are not allowed on the expressways.

cells.v: **Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999**

blocks.text: First Indian product to receive GI Tag = **Darjeeling Tea (2004)**.
Highest number of GI Tags = **Uttar Pradesh (75)**, followed by **Tamil Nadu (58)**.

items: Geographical Indications are used for products that have a specific geographical origin.
In India, GI Tag is governed by the **Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999**.
The law governing Geographical Indication tags came into force on **September 15, 2003**.
The first Indian product to receive GI Tag is **Darjeeling Tea (2004)**.
GI Tag is given for every **10 years**. After this it has to be renewed.
The state receiving the highest number of GI Tags is **Uttar Pradesh (75)** followed by **Tamil Nadu (58)**.

blocks.text: Important facts related to the census of India and the world:-

items: There is mention of census in the book (**Ain-e-Akbari**) written by **Abul Fazal** during the Mughal period.
The credit of conducting the first census in the world using modern methods goes to **Sweden (year 1749 AD)**.
The credit of conducting the first decennial census goes to **America (year 1790 AD)**.
Census in India started in the year **1872** during the tenure of **Mayo**.
Regular census in India started in the year **1881** during the tenure of **Ripon**.
Census is a subject of the Union List under **Article 246** in the **Seventh Schedule** of the Indian Constitution.
The first Census Act after independence was **1948**.
Keeping in view the importance of census work, in the year **1961 AD**, the Census Department was established under the Central Home Ministry.
The census year of **1921 AD** is known as the **great dividing year** in the demographic history of India.
The Census of 2011 was the **7th census** of independent India.
The Census of 2011 was the **15th decennial census**.
The Census of 2011 was the **second census of the 21st century**.
According to the census data of the year 2011, the total population of India is **1,21,08,54,977 (about 121.08 crores)**.
According to the census data of the year 2011, of the total population of India, **51.47%** are males and **48.53%** are females.
According to the census data of the year 2011, the total population of India is approximately **17.5%** of the total population of the world.

According to the census data of the year 2011, the total population of Scheduled Caste (SC) out of the total population of India is approximately **20.137 crores** which is **16.6%** of the total population of the country.

According to the census data of the year 2011, the total population of Scheduled Tribes (ST) out of the total population of India is approximately **10.42 crores** which is **8.6%** of the total population of the country.
The motto of Census 2011 was **'Our Census, Our Future'**.
The first census of independent India started in the year **1951**.
At the time of the first census of independent India, the Registrar General and Census Commissioner of India was **R.A. Gopalaswami**.
The Registrar General and Census Commissioner of India at the time of the 2011 census was **Dr. C. Chandramouli**.

items: CBI-Central Bureau of Investigation:- The Central Bureau of Investigation was established in the year **1963**. Its headquarters is in **New Delhi**. The current CBI director is **Praveen Sood**.
NIA - National Investigation Agency:- NIA was formed under the **National Investigation Agency Act, 2008**.

It is headquartered in **New Delhi**. It is the central agency for investigating and prosecuting crimes related to national security, smuggling of counterfeit Indian currency, human trafficking, nuclear weapons, special crimes, etc.
The current NIA director is **Sadanand Date**.

IB-Intelligence Bureau:- The Intelligence Bureau was established during the British rule and was reorganized under the Home Ministry in **1947**. Its headquarters is in **New Delhi**. The current IB Chief is **Tapan Deka**.

RAW-Research and Analysis Wing:- RAW is India's important international intelligence agency. Its headquarters is in **New Delhi**. IB could not work well in the India-China war of **1962** and the India-Pakistan war of **1965**. Therefore, the Indian government has created a capable and independent agency Wing RAW to collect international information.
It was established on **21 September 1968**. Its headquarters is in **New Delhi**.
Current RAW Director is **Ravi Sinha**.

rows: The size of a ballistic missile is very large.
The size of a cruise missile is small.
Ballistics can carry with it larger and heavier bombs or explosions.
It is capable of carrying very low weight bombs
It is at a very high altitude from the earth, hence it is not easy to hide it from radar
This missile flies at a height of only 15 to 20 meters above the earth's surface, hence it cannot be detected by radar.
This missile follows a semi-circular path and hits its target
This missile travels parallel to the earth's surface and hits the target
Ballistic missiles can keep fuel in reserve.
Fuel cannot be kept in reserve in this.

items: There were a total of **11 sessions** in the Constituent Assembly after which the Constitution of our country was prepared.
The fifth session of the Constituent Assembly was held on **14-30 August 1947**.
After the implementation of the Indian Constitution on **26 January 1950**, the Constituent Assembly ceased to exist and it functioned as the temporary Parliament of India till 1952.
The first Lok Sabha was formed on **17 April 1952** after the first general election of India.
The first parliamentary session of independent India was convened on **13 May 1952**.
The Indian Constituent Assembly had formed **22 committees** for the creation of the Constitution.
The interim government of India was formed on **2 September 1946** during the time of Jawaharlal Nehru.

items: First meeting: **9 December, 1946** (temporary president Sachchidanand Sinha)

Third meeting: **13 December 1946** (Jawaharlal Nehru presented the objective resolution in the Constituent Assembly), which was unanimously accepted on **22 January 1947**.
The number of women representatives in the Constituent Assembly was **15**.
The number of members in the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly was **7**. The chairman of this committee was Dr. B. R. Ambedkar.

blocks.text: The Constituent Assembly adopted the Indian Constitution on **26 November 1949**.

items: It took **2 years 11 months and 18 days** to create the Constitution of India.
The original Constitution had a preamble, **395 articles** and **8 schedules**

blocks.text: Note: At present, the number of articles in the Indian Constitution is **470+,12 schedules and 25 parts**.

items: The Chairman of the House Committee of the Constituent Assembly of India was **B. Pattabhi Sitaramaiya**.
The echo of the slogan of liberty, equality and fraternity is heard in the preamble of the Indian Constitution, which has been taken from the **French Revolution**.
The Constitution of India has not taken any features from the Constitution of **China**.
The parliamentary form of government is also known as the **Westminster system**.
The name of the Westminster system is derived from the place in Central London where the Parliament of the United Kingdom is located. This system is a democratic system.
The longest written constitution in the world.
A mixture of rigidity and flexibility.
Single citizenship.
Parliamentary form of government.
Integrated and independent judiciary.
Universal adult franchise.
The Citizenship Act in India was passed in **1955**. There are **5 methods** of acquiring citizenship in India-
By birth, hereditary, by registration, by naturalization and by territorial inclusion (by merging a territory into India)
After independence, **562 independent princely states** were integrated into India. The responsibility of integrating these princely states was given to **Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel**.

The Preamble of the Indian Constitution mentions **3 types of justice**: social, economic and political and **5 types of freedom**: freedom of thought, expression, belief, religion and worship and equality of Status and opportunity.
**K.M. Munshi** has described the Preamble as the **'political horoscope of the Constitution'**.
**Thakur Bhargava** called the Preamble the **'soul of the Indian Constitution'**

blocks.text: **21st** Amendment added **Sindhi** as the **15th language** in the **Eighth Schedule**

rows: The state was given the right to make special provisions for the development of socially and economically backward classes. Laws related to land reforms were introduced through inserting **Ninth Schedule** and the provision of the judicial review of these laws was included

Common High Courts for two or more states. **14 States** and **6 Union Territories** were created (after abolishing four categories of States.) Extension of the jurisdiction of High Courts to Union Territories.
**Dadra and Nagar Haveli** was included in the Indian Union.
**Goa and Daman and Diu** were included in the Indian Union.
**Nagaland** to be given the status of a state and special provisions made for it.
**Puducherry** was included in the Indian Union.
The retirement age of High Court judges was increased from **60 to 62 years**.
**Sindhi** language included as the **15th language** in the **Eighth Schedule** of the Indian Constitution.
Parliament has the power to amend any part of the Indian Constitution (including the Fundamental Rights).
Curtailment of fundamental right to property.
Abolition of the **Privy Purse** and the privileges of the former rulers of the provincial territories.
The number of Lok Sabha seats was increased from **525 to 545** .

The Protectorate states system of Sikkim was abolished and Sikkim became an associate state of the Indian Union. The 10th Schedule was added for the service conditions of Sikkim state joining the Indian Union.
Sikkim was included as a full state in the Indian Union and 10th Schedule abolished.
The age limit of members of the State Public Service Commission and Joint Public Service Commission was increased from 60 to 62 years.
**42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976** (It is also known as the Mini Constitution.)

Fundamental duties were incorporated. - Three new words included in the Indian Constitution (**Socialist, Secular and Integrity.**) Transfer of **Forest and Education** subjects from State List to Concurrent List.

blocks.text: **61st** Amendment reduced the voting age from **21 to 18** years

rows: The right to property was removed from the list of fundamental rights and given the status of a legal right. In the context of national emergency, the term 'armed rebellion' was used instead of 'internal disturbance'. During the national emergency, the fundamental rights given in **Article 20 and Article 21** will not be suspended.

(Also known as **Anti-Defection Law.** Under this, the **10th Schedule** was included in the Indian Constitution. Provision for disqualifying the members of Parliament and State Legislature in case of defection.
The authorized text of the Constitution was made available in the **Hindi** language and the Hindi text of the Constitution was given equal legal recognition.
The age limit for voting in Lok Sabha and Assembly elections was reduced from **21 to 18 years**.
Delhi was declared as 'National Capital Territory' and provision for a **70-member Legislative Assembly** and a **7-member Council of Ministers** for Delhi.
**Nepali, Manipuri and Konkani** languages included in the Eighth Schedule of the Indian Constitution.

Under this, the **11th Schedule** was included in the Indian Constitution. **Panchayati Raj** institutions were given constitutional status. 29 functions of Panchayats were added to the 11th Schedule.
Under this, the **12th Schedule** was included in the Indian Constitution. Constitutional status was given to urban local bodies. 18 functions of municipalities added to the 12th Schedule.
Ban on re-determination of Lok Sabha and state assembly seats till the year **2026**.
Article 21 (a) states that 'the State shall provide free and compulsory education for children between the ages of **6 and 14**.' (Elementary education included as a fundamental right.)

The maximum number of ministers in the Union Council of Ministers, including the Prime Minister, shall not exceed **15%** of the total number of members of the Lok Sabha. The maximum number of ministers including the Chief Minister in any state will not exceed **15%** of the total number of members of the Legislative Assembly. (The minimum number of ministers in the state including the Chief Minister can be **12**.)
**Bodo, Dogri, Maithili and Santhali** languages included in the Eighth Schedule of the Indian Constitution.
Empowers the State to make special provisions for reservation in educational institutions for the socially and educationally backward classes, Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.

blocks.text: **101st** Constitutional Amendment Act — **Goods and Services Tax (GST)**

rows: Acquisition of some land by India and transfer of some other land to **Bangladesh**.
**National Backward Classes Commission** was given constitutional status.
- Reservation of up to **10%** for **economically weaker sections (EWS)**.

The special representation of the Anglo-Indian community through the nomination process in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies was abolished. - Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) in the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies has been extended for a period of ten years (till the year **2030**).

The State Government was exempted from consulting the National Commission for Backward Classes in respect of preparing and maintaining a list of socially and educationally backward classes for its own purposes.
Reservation of **1/3 seats** of Lok Sabha, State Legislative Assemblies, and the legislative Assembly of the National Capital Territory of Delhi for **Women**.

blocks.text: After the announcement of the Election Commission of India on **March 16, 2024**, Lok Sabha elections for the formation of the **18th Lok Sabha** were held across the country in **7 phases from April 19 to June 1, 2024 (44 days)**. The results of the election were declared on **June 4, 2024**. In which the **Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) won 240 seats** and the BJP-led alliance **NDA (National Democratic Alliance) won a total of 293 seats**. In the opposition, the **Congress Party won 99 seats** and the Congress-led **India Alliance (INDIA-Indian National Developmental Inclusive Alliance) won 234 seats**. Other parties won 10 and independent parties won a total of 7 seats.
Along with the Lok Sabha elections 2024, assembly elections of four states (**Arunachal Pradesh, Odisha, Andhra Pradesh, Sikkim**) were also held.

The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) was established on **April 1, 1935** in **Kolkata** but in **1937** it was moved to **Mumbai**.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) was nationalized on **January 1, 1949**.
The accounting year of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) used to be from **1 July to 30 June**, which has been changed to **1 April to 31 March** from the year **2021**.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) acts as the banker, agent and advisor to the Government of India and the states.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is responsible for the regulation, supervision and control of all banking activities.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has the authority to regulate microfinance in India.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has the power to issue currency in India, regulate it, destroy it if it is no longer operational and also manages the foreign currency reserve in the country.
RBI ensures availability of adequate liquidity in the entire financial system and private banks on a daily basis and acts as the lender of last resort for all the banks.

items: The United Nations was established in **1945** as an international organization. The main objectives of the United Nations are to prevent elements opposing international peace and security, to resolve international disputes peacefully and to establish friendly relations between nations.

At present, the United Nations has **193 member nations**. South Sudan became the **193rd member in 2011**. The list of permanent members of the UN Security Council includes **China, Russia, France, UK and USA**.
India is one of the founding members of the United Nations. On **June 26, 1945**, India signed the Charter of the United Nations along with other participating countries.
After ratifying the United Nations Charter, India joined the United Nations on **October 30, 1945**.
Official languages of the United Nations are **English and French**.
A total of **6 languages** (English, French, Russian, Chinese, Arabic and Spanish) are recognized.
At the time of establishment of the United Nations, only **four languages** were approved (Chinese, English, French, Russian)
Arabic was added in the year **1973**.
New proposed languages for the official languages of the United Nations are **Bengali, Hindi, Portuguese and Turkish**.

The flag of the United Nations is light blue and the symbol of the United Nations is made in white color on it. This symbol has two olive branches which are open upwards. And in between them is the map of the world.

blocks.text: Current Secretary General: **Antonio Guterres** (Former PM of Portugal)
Main organs of the United Nations

items: (i) General Assembly
(ii) Security Council
(iii) Economic and Social Council
(iv) Trusteeship Council
(v) International Court of Justice
(vi) Secretariat

blocks.text: Apart from the International Court of Justice located in **Hague, Netherlands**, all the organs of the United Nations are in the headquarters located in **New York**.

items: (i) General Assembly: It includes representatives of all 193 countries. Therefore, it is also called a **mini parliament**. The President of the General Assembly is elected for **1 year**. Currently, the President (79th) of UNGA is **Philemon Yang**, the former Prime Minister of Cameroon)
A **two-thirds majority** is required for important decisions in the General Assembly such as peace and security, inclusion of new members and budget etc.
At the beginning of the session, the General Assembly elects a new President and **21 Vice-Presidents**.
Apart from the regular session, the General Assembly can also organize a special session on the recommendation of the Security Council.

On the recommendation of the Security Council, the appointment of the judge of the International Court of Justice, membership of new countries, appointment of the Secretary General and passing the budget of the United Nations are the functions of the General Assembly.

(ii) Security Council (UNSC): It is the main executive organ of the UNO. Maintaining security is the main responsibility of the Security Council, so it is also called the **'policeman of the world'**. It has **15 members**. **5 permanent members (veto power): China, Russia, France, UK and USA**. While **10 temporary members** are elected by the General Assembly for two years, whose tenure is 2 years. America first used the veto in the **1970s** decade.The Soviet Union (Russia) has used the veto the most number of times.

United Nations Human Rights Council: The United Nations Human Rights Council was formed by the United Nations General Assembly in the year **2006**. It replaced the Human Rights Commission which was earlier under the Economic and Social Council. Its headquarters is in **Geneva (Switzerland)**. Currently its President is **Omar zniber**.

blocks.text: Note: The current chairman of the National Human Rights Commission is **Arun Kumar Mishra**.
(iii) Economic and Social Council: It has 54

pages.topic: Famous International Organisations and their Headquarters

items: members elected by the General Assembly, whose tenure is **3 years**. Every year the Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) holds two meetings in New York in April and Geneva in July.
The current President of the Economic and Social Council is **Bob Rai (80th)**.
This Council functions through many various Special agencies such as : WHO, WTO, World Bank, UNICEF, and UNESCO,on Economic,Social and Environment related issues.

(iv) Trusteeship Council: The responsibility of the Trusteeship Council has been entrusted to **Australia, New Zealand, Britain and America**. At present, the number of member countries in the Trusteeship Council is **12**, which includes 4 managers, 3 permanent members and 5 elected members. The functions of the Trusteeship Council ended with the independence of the Pacific island **Palau in 1994**.
(v) International Court of Justice (ICJ):
It was established in **June, 1945** and started functioning in **April, 1946** and its headquarters is in the **Peace Palace located in The Hague (Netherlands)**.
There are 5 chapters in the legal book of the International Court of Justice.
It has **15 judges**, who are appointed for **9 years**. There cannot be two judges from the same country in it. The quorum number of the court is **9**.
The first Indian judge in the International Court of Justice was **Benegal Rama Rao**. The first Indian President of the International Court of Justice was **Nagendra Singh**.
The Indian Judge in the International Court of Justice- **Dalveer Bhandari(2018-2027)**.

(vi) Secretariat: The Secretariat deals with the day-to-day work of the United Nations. Its head is the **Secretary General**, who is appointed by the General Assembly on the recommendation of the Security Council for a term of **5 years**. The Secretary General can also be appointed for a second term. According to the charter, the Secretary General is the chief administrative officer of the organization. The first Secretary General of the United Nations was **Trygveli (Norway)**. **Dag Hammarskjöld** was the Secretary General who died in an air crash.

items: The annual Academy Award given by the American Academy of National Motion Picture Arts and Sciences is also called **'Oscar Award'**.
In this, the best directors, actors, writers and technicians associated with the film industry are honored.
This award ceremony was held on **May 16, 1929**.
Ben Hur (1959), Titanic (1997) and The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003) have all won **11 Oscars** each.

Its official name is **'Academy Award of Merit'**.
This award is organised every year in the month of February or March.
There are 2 people who have received both Oscar and Nobel Prize. **George Bernard Shaw and Bob Dylan**.

George Bernard Shaw was awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature (**1925**) and the Oscar Award for Best Screen Play in **1938** and Bob Dylan was awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature in **2016** and the Oscar Award in **2000** for his song 'In One Afternoon'.
India's 'Satyajit Ray' was given the Oscar's **'Lifetime Award' in 1992**. He was the first Indian to receive this award for his achievements in cinema.

Prize and money: The Oscar award winner is given an Oscar statuette. This statuette is covered with gold and the recipients are made to sign an agreement that they will not sell it. The trophy given to the winners is not accompanied by any cash or other items.

items: The Bharat Ratna is awarded for **national service**. The award was originally limited to arts, literature, science, public service and sports but in **December 2011** the government expanded the criteria to include any field of human endeavour.
This award was established on **January 2, 1954** by the then President of India, **Shri Rajendra Prasad**.
The first recipients of this award were **Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, C. Rajagopalachari and C.V. Raman**, who were honored in the year **1954**.
From the year **1955**, provision for giving honour posthumously was added.
The first posthumous Bharat Ratna award was given to former Prime Minister of India **Shri Lal Bahadur Shastri** in the year **1966**.
Bharat Ratna could be awarded to a maximum of **three** persons in a year.
This rule was broken for the first time in the year

blocks.text: **Sachin Tendulkar** is the only sportsperson till now to receive the Bharat Ratna and the youngest person (**40 years**) to receive it.

items: 1999, when this award was given to four people - **Amartya Sen, Jayaprakash Narayan, Ravi Shankar and Gopinath Bordoloi**.

In the year **2024**, this rule was again broken and this award was given to **five** persons - **Lal Krishna Advani, Karpoori Thakur** (posthumously). - **Chaudhary Charan Singh** (posthumously), **P.V. Narasimha Rao** (posthumously) and **Dr. M.S. Swaminathan** (posthumously)
Till now **53** people have been awarded the Bharat Ratna Award in various fields.
Description: Bharat Ratna inscribed in **Devanagari** script with a **platinum image of the Sun** on a **Peepal leaf**.
Sachin Tendulkar is the only sportsperson till now, to have received the Bharat Ratna and he is the youngest person (40 years) to receive this honour.
Award: Those who receive Bharat Ratna receive a certificate and a medal from the Government of India. No money or amount is given with this honor.

cells.v: For **distinguished service of a high order**.

items: Padma Awards are considered among the honours given by the Government of India for notable contributions.
Padma Awards are given in **three** categories-
Padma Vibhushan — For exceptional and distinguished service.
Padma Bhushan — For distinguished service of a high order.
Padma Shri — For distinguished service
Due to change in government, Bharat Ratna Awards and Padma Awards were suspended from **July, 1977 to January, 1980** and again from **August, 1992 to December, 1995**.
Padma awards are given in the fields of medicine, literature, sports, arts, social service, education, science, engineering, civil services etc.
In the year **2024**, **132** people have been honoured with Padma Awards, in which **110** people have been

blocks.text: nominated for **Padma Shri**, **17** people for **Padma Bhushan** and **5** people for **Padma Vibhushan**.

items: Padma Vibhushan award is the **second highest Civilian Award** given by the Government of India, which is given for valuable contribution in various fields for the country. This award is given by the **President of India**.
Established: January 2,1954
This is the second most prestigious award of India after **Bharat Ratna**.
First decoration: Year 1954
First Decorated Person: Satyendra Nath Bose and others
This honor is given for distinguished and remarkable service in any field. It also includes services rendered by government employees.
1. Shri Duvvur Nageshwar Reddy (Medicine)
2. Justice (Retd.) Shri Jagdish Singh Khehar (Public Affairs)
3. Smt. Kumudini Rajnikant Lakhia (Art)
4. Shri LakshminarayanaSubramaniam (Art)
5. Shri M. T. Vasudevan Nair (Posthumous) (Literature and Education)
6. Smt. Sharda Sinha (Posthumous) (Art)
7. Shri Osamu Suzuki (Posthumous) (Trade and Industry)
It is the **third highest civilian honour** given by the Government of India.
This award is given for distinguished service of high order, without any distinction of occupation, position, caste and Gender.
Established: January 2,1954
First honored - Year 1954
Padma Bhushan award was given to **19** people in the year **2025**.
Award: A **bronze badge** is given as an honour. It has a **lotus flower** in the middle, with three leaves surrounding it. A certificate signed by the President is given.
It is the **fourth highest civilian award** given by the Government of India. It was announced on **26 January**.
Established: Year-1954
First decoration: 1954
First awardee: Balbir Singh Dosanjh, 1954
The first woman to receive the Padma Shri award was **Nargis Dutt**.
In the year **2025**, Padma Shri Award was given to **113** people.
