cells.v: India's **highest military medal awarded in peace time**

items: Ashoka Chakra is India's **highest military medal awarded in peace time**. This award is given to soldiers and civilians for extraordinary bravery, valor or sacrifice.
It can also be given posthumously.
Ashoka Chakra is awarded by the **President**.
This medal is awarded for displaying commendable acts of courage, valour and self-sacrifice on land, water and sky.
Established: January 4,1952
First awarded: January 26,1952
On **January 26, 2022**, **ASI Babu Ram** of Jammu and Kashmir Police was awarded the Ashok Chakra posthumously.
Total recipients: 90 (58 posthumously)

blocks.text: **B.B.L. Dutt** was the first civilian to receive the Kirti Chakra (in the year **1952**).

items: This award for gallantry is given to the brave man who has displayed extraordinary courage in fighting the enemy.
This honour is given to soldiers and civilians for extraordinary bravery or sacrifice.
It can also be given posthumously.
It is the **second highest military medal awarded in peace time**.
B.B.L. Dutt was the first civilian to receive the Kirti Chakra (in the year 1952).
Established: As **Ashoka Chakra-II** on **4 January 1952** but came into existence as Kirti Chakra in **January 1967**.
Two Kirti Chakras were awarded in the year **2025** (Posthumous): **Major Manjit and Naik Dilwar Khan**.
**Captain Joginder Singh Gharaya and 11 others** were the first recipients of the Kirti Chakra.
It is awarded to both armed forces personnel and civilians for exceptional bravery.
**14** Shaurya Chakra (3 Posthumously)- in **2025**

cells.v: medal, **Rs 1 lakh**, a certificate and a citation

items: This award is given by the **President of India** every year in the week before **Republic Day**. This award is given to children in the age group of **5 to 18 years**.
Award: A medal, a cash prize of **Rs 1 lakh**, a certificate and a citation are given.
This award is given for exceptional achievement in **7** categories (Bravery, Art and Culture, Environment, Innovation, Science and Technology, Social Service and Sports).
In **2024**, this award has been given to **17** children.
It is India's **highest military decoration** awarded for display of exceptional courage and indomitable and extraordinary acts of bravery during war.
This medal is made of **bronze** on which **Indravajra** is inscribed on one side.
The first Paramvir Chakra in India was awarded to **Major Somnath Sharma** on **June 21, 1950**, posthumously for military action (year 1947).
The last person to receive the Param Vir Chakra was **Rifleman Sanjay Kumar** of **13th Jammu and Kashmir Rifles** for **Operation Vijay** in the Kargil conflict (**1999**).
Established: January 26, 1950
Total recipients: 21 (14 posthumously)
This is the **second highest gallantry award** of the country.
Mahavir Chakra is a medal given in India for display of bravery during war.
It can also be given posthumously.
Established: January 26,1950
First awarded: **Diwan Singh Danu, 1947** (posthumously)
This honour is the country's **third highest wartime gallantry award** after the Paramvir Chakra and the Mahavir Chakra.
Established: January 26,1950

cells.v: **Ustad Abdul Halim Zafar Khan** (Sitar Player Utsad, 2000)

items: Tansen Festival or Tansen Music Festival is celebrated every year in the month of **December** in **Gwalior, Madhya Pradesh**.
The festival is organised by the Culture Department of the Madhya Pradesh Government near the **tomb of Tansen**.
The prestigious 'National Tansen Samman' is a music award given to exponents of **Hindustani music**.
Establishment: Year,1980
Prize: Rs 5 lakh
First Tansen Award: Ustad Abdul Halim Zafar Khan (Sitar Player Utsad, 2000)
Tansen Samman 2023: Pt. Ganapati Bhatt Hasnagi (Classical Singer)
Centenary year of Tansen Samaroh in the year **2024** organized from **15th to 19th December, 2024**.
Tansen Samman 2024: Pt. Swapan Choudhary

cells.v: **Dr. Harivansh Rai Bachchan** (Kya Bhooloon Kya Yaad Karoon)
**Rs. 15 lakhs**, a citation, statue of Goddess Saraswati

items: Saraswati Samman is a literary award given by **K.K. Birla Foundation**.
This award is given every year for an outstanding literary work published in the **22** languages listed in the **Eighth Schedule** of the Indian Constitution.
Saraswati Samman was started in the year **1991**.
The first Saraswati Samman was given to the Hindi writer **Dr. Harivansh Rai Bachchan** for his four-volume autobiography (Kya Bhooloon Kya Yaad Karoon).
It is given in the field of **literature**.
Saraswati Samman **2023 (33rd)** awarded to **Prabha Varma** (Malayalam) for her major poetry collection 'Raudra Satvikam'.
Under this award a sum of **Rs. 15 lakhs** and a citation along with a statue of Goddess Saraswati is given.

cells.v: **Rs 25 lakh** in cash, a trophy and a citation

items: The Indira Gandhi Peace Prize is given in the memory of former Prime Minister of India **Indira Gandhi**. Since **1986**, the Indira Gandhi Peace, Disarmament and Development Prize is awarded every year by the 'Indira Gandhi Memorial Trust' established in her memory to a person in the world who has made a significant contribution in the work of social service, disarmament or development.
Established: Year,1986
First recipient: Parliamentarians for Global Action (1986)
Recipients of 2023: Daniel Barenboim (pianist) and Ali Abu Awwad (Israel-Palestine) for non-violent resolution of conflict Latest
Indira Gandhi Peace Prize 2024- Michelle Bachelet
Prize money: Rs 25 lakh in cash, a trophy and a citation
Miss India or Femina Miss India Award is given once a year.
Only after becoming the winner in this, the Indian beauty is allowed to participate in the international beauty contest.
Established: Year 1952 (Headquarters Mumbai)
Official languages: Hindi, English
First winner: Esther Victoria Abraham (Pramila), in **1947**
The title of the **60th** edition of Miss India Awards in the year **2024** was won by **Nikita Porwal**, she is from 'Mahakal City' Ujjain of Madhya Pradesh.
This award was started in the year **2018**.
This award is given to Indian writers for novels, poetry, short stories and plays.
In the year **2023**, **Sukrita Paul Kumar** was awarded the sixth Rabindranath Tagore Literary Award for her book 'Salt and Pepper'.
Jawaharlal Nehru Goodwill Award is an international award given by the Government of India, established in the year **1965**.

This award is given by the **Indian Council of Cultural Relations (ICCR)** of the Government of India for outstanding contribution in the field of international understanding, disarmament and development.
Established: Year,1965
First recipient: U Thant (1965)
Last recipient: Angela Merkel (Germany) in 2009.
This award was not given to anyone between the years **1986 and 1995 to 2003**.
Prize money: Rs 2.5 million (Rs 25 lakh), a citation.
This award was started in the year **1954** for contribution in the field of literature and language.
This award is given for all the **22** languages included in the **8th Schedule** of the Indian Constitution and a total of **24** languages including English and Rajasthani.
Its first president was Jawaharlal Nehru.
First awarded: **1955, Makhanlal Chaturvedi**, work 'Him Tarangini' (collection of poems)
Sahitya Akademi Award for the year **2023** was given to **Sanjeev** for the Hindi novel 'Mujhe Pehchano'.
Award (Amount): **Rs.1 Lakh** in cash, shawl and citation along with a copper plate.

This award was established in the year **2011**, which is given every year to young writers doing excellent work in **22** major languages (languages included in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution) along with English and Rajasthani languages.
The purpose of establishing this award is to encourage young writers under the age of **35** in the field of literature.
The award carries a cash prize of **Rs 50,000** and an engraved copper plaque.

This award for the year **2024** in the field of Hindi language was given to **Gaurav Pandey** for his poetry collection 'Smritiyon Ke Beech Ghiri Hai Prithvi'. Apart from this, for the English language, this award was given to **K. Vaishali** for her memoir book 'Homeless: Growing Up Lesbian and Dyslexic in India'.

This award was established in the year **2010**, which is given every year in **22** major languages (languages included in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution) along with English and Rajasthani languages.

blocks.text: This award carries a cash prize of **Rs 50,000** and an engraved copper plaque.

items: This award was given to **Devendra Kumar** in the field of Hindi language for the year **2024** for his story collection '51 Bal Kahaniyan'.
In addition, this award was given to **Nandini Sen Gupta** for the English Language for her story collection "'The Blue Horse and Other Amazing Animals from Indian History'.

blocks.text: Major Dhyanchand Khel Ratna Award (formerly known as Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award)

cells.v: a medal, a citation and **Rs 25 lakh**

items: Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award is the **highest Sports award** given in India.

This award is named after the best hockey player of India and world, who was a member of the three-time Olympic gold medal winning Indian hockey team (**Amsterdam Olympics in 1928, Los Angeles Olympics in 1932 and Berlin Olympics in 1936**).

It is selected every year by a committee constituted by the **Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports** and is awarded for the spectacular and most outstanding performance in the field of sports at the international level during the preceding four year period.
Establishment: Year 1991-92
First decoration: Year 1991 (Winner Vishwanath Anand)
Award: This award consists of a medal, a citation and a cash prize of Rs 25 lakh to the awardee.
1. Manu Bhaker (Shooting)
2. Praveen Kumar (Para-Athletics)
3. Harmanpreet Singh (Hockey)
4. Gukesh Dommaraju (Chess)
Kalinga Award is given to people who have made significant contribution in promoting and popularizing science among the society.
Kalinga Award is given for outstanding contribution in popularizing science
This award is given by **UNESCO**.
The first Indian to be awarded the Kalinga Award is **Jagjit Singh**, who received this award as a writer in **1963**.
The award is **biennial** and is presented on **World Science Day** on **10 November**.
First recipient: Louis de Broglie, 1952
In the year **2023** this award was given to **Ana Mario Cetto (Mexico)**.

cells.v: **Government of India** (lifetime contribution to Indian cinema)
**Rs 10 lakh**, a Golden Lotus Medal and a shawl

items: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is an annual award given by the Government of India to an individual for his/her lifetime contribution to Indian cinema.
This award was started in the year **1969**, the birth centenary year of Dada Saheb Phalke.
This award is given by the **Ministry of Information and Broadcasting**, Government of India to the person who has made significant contribution to the development of Indian cinema.
Established: Year,1969
First recipient: Devika Rani Rorick (1969)
Award: Rs 10 lakh, a Golden Lotus Medal and a shawl.
In the year **2023**, this honor has been given to **Waheeda Rehman** (53rd edition) and in the year **2024** to **Mithun Chakraborty** (54th edition).

blocks.text: India's first Arjuna Award winner was **Manuel Aaron (1961)**, India's first Chess International Master.
First woman to receive the Arjuna Award: **Kumari Meena Shah (1962)**.

items: Arjuna Award is an award given to sportspersons by the Government of India for outstanding performance in the field of sports.
The Arjuna Award was started in **1961**.
Arjuna Award is given to a player who performs outstandingly for **four** consecutive years.
From the year **2020**, the winner of this award receives a prize money of `15 lakh, a bronze statue of Arjuna and a citation.
India's first Arjuna Award winner was Manuel Aaron (1961) who was India's first Chess International Master.
The first woman to receive the Arjuna Award was Kumari Meena Shah (1962).
First honored- year, 1961
In **2024** **34** Athletes Received the Arjuna Award
Sucha Singh-Athletics
Murlikant Petkar– Para-Swimming
Dronacharya Award, known as Dronacharya Award for outstanding contribution in sports and games. This award is named after **Guru Dronacharya**.
This award is given every year by the **Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports**.
Established: Year,1985
Category: Coaches in Sports (Individual)
First decoration: 1985 (by Government of India)
First winners: 1. Bhalchandra Bhaskar Bhagwat (Wrestling) 2. Om Prakash Bhardwaj (Boxing) 3. O.M. Nambiar (Athletics)
First female award winner: Hansa Sharma (Weightlifting) in the year 2002
Regular Category
Subhash Rana – Para-Shooting
Deepali Deshpande- Shooting
Sandeep Sangwan - Hockey
Lifetime Category
S Muralidharan - Badminton
Armando Colaco- Football
Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award, formerly known as National Adventure Award. It is the **highest adventure sports honour** of the Republic of India.
The award is named after **Tenzing Norgay**, who was one of the first two men to reach the summit of **Mount Everest** along with **Edmund Hillary** in **1953**.
This award is given every year by the **Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports**.
The recipient is honoured for outstanding achievement in the field of adventure sports on land, water or air during the previous three years.
It is provided by the Government of India.
Established in the year **1993-1994**, these awards were first given in the year **1994**. This award is considered equivalent to **Arjuna Award**.

items: The National emblem of India was taken from the **Ashoka Pillar of Sarnath**.
The original pillar has **four lions** at the top. Lion is a symbol of courage and boldness.
In the middle of the strip is the **Dharmachakra**. The **24 spokes** in the wheel symbolize the 24 hours.
It was adopted on **26 January 1950**.
A **bull** on the right side of the Wheel symbolises agricultural economy and a **horse** on the left side of the Wheel symbolises indomitable power, hard work and dynamism.
Under the panel, the phrase '**Satyameva Jayate**' of the **Mundaka Upanishad** is written in **Devanagari script**.
Adopted by the Constituent Assembly on **22 July 1947**.
The ratio of its length and breadth is **3:2**.
The National Flag has three stripes of **saffron, white and green**.
In the centre of the white stripe there is a **dark blue circle with 24 spokes**.
**Jana-Gana-Mana** was adopted as the national anthem by the Indian Constituent Assembly on **January 24, 1950**.
Originally written in **Bengali** by **Rabindranath Tagore**.
It was first sung on **27 December 1911** at the **26th session** of the Indian National Congress (Kolkata).
The duration of singing the national anthem is **52 seconds**.
'**Vande Mataram**' was adopted as the national song by the Indian Constituent Assembly on **January 24, 1950**.
It was composed by **Bankim Chandra Chatterjee** in **Sanskrit**.
It has a status equivalent to Jana-Gana-Mana.
It was first sung in the **12th session** of the Congress (Kolkata) in the year **1896**.
The National Calendar was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on **22 March 1957**.
Along with the Gregorian calendar, the country also has a national almanac based on the **Shak Samvat**. Its first month is **Chaitra**.
The national animal of India is **Tiger** (Panthera Tigris).
To stop the decreasing number of tigers in the country, **Project Tiger** was started in **April 1973**. Under this, many tiger reserves were established in the country.
The first National park of India is **Jim Corbett National Park**.
The national bird of India is the **Indian Peacock** (Pavo cristatus).
In the year **1963**, Peacock was declared the national bird.
Peacock enjoys full protection under the **Wildlife Protection Act 1972**.
**Lotus** (Nelumbo Nucifera) is the national flower of India.
It is described in Indian art and mythological literature.
The state flower of **Haryana and Karnataka** is lotus.
The national fruit of India is **Mango** (Mangifera Indica).
Mango is also the national fruit of **Pakistan and Philippines**.
Ganga river is the **longest river in India**. Its total length is **2525 km**.
Its origin is from the **Gangotri Glacier**.
This river together with the Brahmaputra River forms the world's largest delta, the **Sundarban Delta**.
In Bangladesh the Ganges River is known as **Padma River**.
This river flows through **five states** of India (Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal).

blocks.text: Note:- **Ganges Dolphin** (Susu) was declared the National Aquatic Animal in the year **2009**. Dolphin Day is celebrated every year on **5 October**.

items: The **Asian elephant** has the status of National Heritage Animal.
The Asian elephant was declared National Heritage Animal in **October, 2010**.
World Elephant Day is celebrated on **12 August**.
The state animal of **Kerala, Karnataka and Jharkhand** is elephant.
The Indian Rupee got a new symbol on **July 15, 2010** like the dollar, yen, euro and pound.
The new symbol of rupee (`) is created by combining '`' of **Devanagari script** and (R) of **Roman script**.

items: Ancient Olympic Games were played in **776 BC** in **Olympia, Greece**. For the first time these games were played in honour of the Greek God **Zeus**. After that, due to the order of the Roman king **Theodosius**, the organization of these games was stopped.
The modern Olympic Games competition began in **1896** in **Athens, Greece**, with the efforts of **Pierre de Coubertin of France**. It is organized every **4 years**.

The International Olympic Committee was established in the year **1894**. Its headquarters is in **Lausanne (Switzerland)**. It has a Director General, a President, **4 Vice Presidents** and **10 other members**.
The lighting of the Olympic torch began in **1928** from the **Amsterdam Olympics**.
The practice of taking oath at Olympic Games began with the **Antwerp Olympic Games in 1920**.
The introduction of mascots in the Olympic Games began with the **1968 Mexico City Olympics**.
In the march past at the opening ceremony of the Olympics, the **Greek team comes first** and the **host country's team comes last**, the rest of the teams come in the middle.
The celebration of Olympic Day began in **1948**. It is celebrated on **23 June**.

blocks.text: New sports included in Tokyo Olympics 2020
New sports have been included in Tokyo Olympics 2020. Which are the following-

items: 1. Skateboarding
2. Baseball/ Softball
3. Sport climbing
4. Karate
5. Surfing

The flag was first used in the **Antwerp Olympic Games in 1920**. In the middle of the flag, **five circles** are shown joined to each other, which represent the **5 continents** of the world. The Olympic rings were first publicly presented in the year **1913**.
So there are a total of **6 colours** in the Olympic flag. It was designed by **Coubertin of France**.
The first Indian woman member of the International Olympic Committee is **Nita Ambani**.
The current President of the International Olympic Committee is **Thomas Bach (Germany)**.

The male athlete who has won the most gold medals in a single Olympics is **Michael Phelps of the USA**. Known as the 'Golden Shark', the flying fish and the Baltimore Bullet. Phelps won **8 gold medals** in the **2008 Beijing Olympics**. So far, he has won a total of **28 medals** including **23 gold, 3 silver and 2 bronze**.
The woman who has won the most gold medals in the Olympic Games is **Larisa Latynina**.

The Indian Olympic Council was temporarily established in **1924**, which was permanently established in **1927** and **Sir Dorabji Tata** was its first President. The current President is **P.T. Usha**.
The first player to participate in the Olympics on behalf of India was an Anglo-Indian **Norman Pritchard** who participated in the Olympics (**1900 AD**).
The first Indian to win an individual gold medal in the Olympic Games is **Abhinav Bindra** (10 m air rifle, Beijing Olympics **2008**).
The first Indian female athlete to participate in the Olympics is **Mary Leela Rao**. (Year **1956**, 100 m race)
The first Indian woman who won a bronze medal for India in Sydney Olympics 2000 is **Karnam Malleswari** and the first Indian woman who won silver medal in Rio Olympics 2016 is **P.V. Sindhu**.

India's flag bearer at the opening ceremony of Tokyo Olympics 2020: **Mary Kom (boxer)** and **Manpreet Singh (captain of the men's hockey team)**, India's flag bearer at the closing ceremony was **Bajrang Punia (wrestler)**.
America ranked first in Tokyo Olympics 2021 and India ranked **48th** (with **1 gold, 2 silver, 4 bronze** medals).
The **32nd** Olympic Games were held in **Tokyo (Japan)** in **2021**.
The **33rd** Olympic Games were be held in **Paris (France)** in **2024**.
The **34th** Olympic Games are scheduled to be held in **Los Angeles (USA)** in **2028**.
The **35th** Olympic Games are scheduled to be held in **Brisbane (Australia)** in the year **2032**.
These Olympic Games are also held at an interval of **four years**. The Winter Olympic Games started in
the year **1924**. The first Winter Olympic Games were played in **Chamonix (France)**.
The Winter Olympic Games were held in **Beijing (China) in 2022**. India participated in them from the year **1964**.
**Norway** -37 (16 gold, 8 silver, 13 bronze)
**Germany**- 27 (12 gold, 10 silver, 5 bronze)
**China** -15 (9 gold, 4 silver, 2 bronze)
Note: Winter Olympics **2026** will be held in **Italy**
The Olympic Games have been cancelled **three times**.
**1916**- Berlin (during World War I)
**1940** -Tokyo (during World War II)
**1944**- London (during World War II)
**31-year-old Arif Khan** was the only Indian to participate in the Beijing Winter Olympics 2022 but he could not win any medal for India.
The Youth Olympics will be held in **Senegal** in the year **2026**.

Paralympic Games is an international sports competition for the differently-abled. The Para Olympic Games were held for the first time in **Rome in 1960**. India joined the Para Olympic Games in the year **1968**.
**Devendra Jhajharia** has won a total of **3 medals** (2004 gold, 2016 gold and 2020 silver) in **javelin throw** in the Paralympics.
**Mariyappan Thangavelu** has won a total of **3 medals** in Paralympics (Gold medal in 2016, Silver medal in 2020 and Bronze medal in 2024) in **Men's High Jump**.
**Deepa Malik** is the first Indian woman to win a Para Olympic medal. (Won silver medal in shot put event at **Rio 2016**)
After the Olympics, it is the biggest sporting event in the world. These games are usually organized between two Olympic Games.
Commonwealth Games started in **1930** in **Hamilton (Canada)**.
India first participated in the **London Games in 1934**.
In the year **2022** Commonwealth Games were held in **Birmingham, England**.

The **23rd** Commonwealth Games to be held in the year 2026 were to be held in **Victoria, Australia** but after Australia's refusal, these games will now be held in **Glasgow, the capital of Scotland**.
India won its first gold medal in the Commonwealth Games in **1958**. This gold medal was won by the great sprinter **Milkha Singh**.
**Rupa Unnikrishnan** became the first Indian woman to win a gold medal at the Commonwealth Games, winning the 50m rifle prone title at the CWG **1998** in **Kuala Lumpur**.
India has won a total of **61 medals** in the Commonwealth Games 2022. This includes **22 gold, 16 silver and 23 bronze** medals.

India's star women's badminton player **PV Sindhu** and men's hockey team captain **Manpreet Singh** were the flag bearers of India for the Commonwealth Games 2022. Table tennis player **Achanta Sharath Kamal** and boxer **Nikhat Zareen** were the flag bearers of India for the closing ceremony.
The Commonwealth Games of the year **2010** were held in **India**.
**Australia** has hosted the Commonwealth Games **five times**.
The Asian Games began on **March 4, 1951** in **New Delhi**.
India again hosted these games in **1982**. India had won **51 medals** in the 1951 Asian Games, which included **15 gold, 16 silver and 20 bronze** medals.
The **shining sun** is the symbol of the Asian Games.
The **2023** Asian Games were held in **Hangzhou, China**, which were scheduled to be played in 2022.
In the year **2026**, the Asian Games will be held in **Aichi Province and Nagoya (Japan)**.
In the year **2030**, the Asian Games will be held in **Doha, the capital of Qatar**.
In the year **2034**, the Asian Games will be held in **Riyadh, the capital of Saudi Arabia**.

blocks.text: However, if both players win three points each in a game (ie the score is 40-40), it is called **deuce**.
The player who wins the next point after deuce gets the **advantage**. After that if he wins the next point, he wins the game.
However, if the opposing player wins the next point after the advantage, the score goes back to deuce. A player team needs to win **two consecutive points** after deuce to win a game.

Mission Indradhanush, representing the seven colours of the rainbow, aims to immunize all children who are not or are partially vaccinated against seven dangerous diseases (diphtheria, pertussis, tetanus, polio, tuberculosis, measles and hepatitis B) by 2020.
Start date year **2017**
To cover 90% of vaccinations for children below 2 years of age.
Effective year **2020-21** (during COVID-19 pandemic)

To expand the coverage of the National Immunization Program, the Central Government launched Intensified Mission Indradhanush (IMI) 3.0 in **February 2020**. The main objective of this mission is to reach out to the unorganized population for their vaccination and also to offer all the available vaccines under the Universal Immunization Program.
This mission covers deadly diseases like polio, measles, tetanus, diphtheria, whooping cough, hepatitis B, Rubella, Japanese encephalitis and pneumonia.
Effective year-**2022**
Under this, more than 3 crore pregnant women and 2.6 crore children are to be covered annually through the Universal Immunization Programme (UIP).

blocks.text: Note: Mission Indradhanush includes the provision of **11 types of vaccines** that enhance protection against preventable diseases. A total of **5.46 crore** children and **1.32 crore** pregnant women have been vaccinated in all phases of Mission Indradhanush in the country till December 2024

The rural roads constructed under this scheme will be in accordance with the provisions of the **Indian Road Congress (IRC)**. (Note- IRC is the apex body of highway engineers in the country, which was established in the year **1934**.)

The Central Government has approved the implementation of **Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana-IV (PMGSY-IV)** from **FY 2024-25 to 2028-29** and under this scheme, financial assistance will be provided for construction of **62,500 km** of roads to provide new connectivity to **25,000 eligible unconnected habitations** with a total outlay of **`70,125 crore** (Central share `49,087.50 crore and State share `21,037.50 crore).

The Scheme will cover **25,000 unconnected habitations** with **500 + population** in plain areas, **250+** in North Eastern and Hilly States/UTs, **100+** in Special Category Areas and Insurgency affected districts, as per **Census 2011**.
